title: Expand-contract for irreversible schema changes
description: Prefer additive, backward-compatible migration steps before removing columns, changing semantics, or rewriting data.
body: Use when database, queue, event, index, or API data shape changes can affect running production code. Do not execute migrations or destructive changes automatically. Actions: add compatible fields first, deploy readers/writers safely, backfill with checks, then remove only after evidence and approval. Validation signals include migration dry-run, compatibility tests, row counts, and rollback or pause plan. Risk boundary: destructive migration or data rewrite requires human gate. Use when Production Change Risk Controls work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
